expectations.text: Explains that a single rename is unsafe during rolling deploys because old code writes to the old column name
expectations.evidence: During a rolling deploy two app versions run at once; old code references email, new references email_address. The instant the migration runs, email no longer exists and every old process querying it throws errors.

expectations.text: Provides a multi-deploy sequence with at least 3 distinct steps/deploys
expectations.evidence: 5-deploy sequence: add column, dual-write, backfill, read from new, stop using old, drop old.

expectations.text: Shows migration code for adding the new column
Shows backfill migration using disable_ddl_transaction! and in_batches
Mentions ignored_columns before dropping the old column
expectations.evidence: Mentions adding column to self.ignored_columns before dropping it in an earlier deploy.

expectations.text: Shows migration code for dropping the old column using safety_assured

suggestions: All 6 passed even without the skill — consider tightening expectations toward skill-specific guidance (strong_migrations deterministic CI check, exact lock-level reasoning, precise naming conventions).
Response went beyond rubric (concurrent unique index, NOT NULL handling, alias_attribute alternative). Add expectations if these are skill-differentiating.
eval_feedback.overall: without_skill response fully satisfied all 6 expectations. Strong baseline — rubric may not distinguish skill from no-skill here. The skill adds structural scaffolding and Wealthbox-specific patterns, but core knowledge is present without it.
